atoms.semantic_claim: The file added a public build-configuration struct carrying base fee, block number, block gas limit, and maximum block size.

atoms.source_snippet:
pub struct UnifiedBuildResult {
    pub cumulative_gas_used: u64,
    pub total_fees: alloy_primitives::U256,
    pub current_block_size: usize,
    pub scheduled_count: usize,
    pub fulfilled_count: usize,
    pub delivery_count: usize,
    pub regular_count: usize,
}
atoms.semantic_claim: The unified build result reports cumulative gas, total fees, current block size, and per-source execution counts for scheduled, fulfilled async, delivery, and regular transactions.

#[test]
fn bucket_sort_reorders_by_priority() { ... }
destinations.evidence: Destination also tests sequencing-rights bucket ordering.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The destination tests cover the replacement delivery fee decoder/formula and sequencing bucket ordering, but there is no direct unit test for the old can_fit_in_block helper because size admission is integrated in payload building.
atoms.notes: Confirmed support/test coverage is non-exact: behavior is present in current helpers/tests, but the old helper-specific unit tests are not carried forward one-for-one.

summary: Mechanical review complete. The old unified_builder.rs module is not present by name, but current payload code now structurally covers its core fulfilled-async/delivery/regular/pool candidate collection, effective-tip sort, stable tie-breaker, block-size check, and sequencing-rights reorder through PayloadCandidate. Remaining confirmed gaps are scheduler-state obligations outside that list, missing full-call-graph sequencing classification, and support-only metrics/per-source counters.
notes: Reviewed against current port after PayloadCandidate landed; no atoms remain in needs-review state.
